findings: Precondition 6 still requires Ask ≠ Bid as a hard blocker (isolates ask vs bid); revised methodology removes this as blocking precondition.
Actions 8–11 contain Compute (steps 8–9) and Compare (steps 10–11), violating Action/Result separation; cognitive work must live in Results only.
Results include two asserted outcomes; counterfactual negative (bid-based mismatch) must be moved to Peculiarities as optional explanation per revised methodology.
Critical checklist: No conditional clauses in Results — PASS (no when/if/provided that in Results bullets).
Critical checklist: No Compute/Calculate/Compare/Verify in Actions 1–7 — PASS for verbs; steps 8–11 violate.
Traceability block format differs from AC-23 (full paths vs abbreviated Test List line only).
Test Items: AC-22 names Current Ask/Bid and read-only Order Value; AC-23 only 'Order Value display' — asymmetry.
Precondition 2 wording: 'Order Entry is open' vs AC-23 'Order Entry popup is open'.
Precondition 4: 'tester can obtain' vs AC-23 'tester can read' — paired wording not mirrored.
Both map to DX-WEB-097 — PASS on requirement reference substance.
Precondition 1 embeds explicit documentation/confirmation requirement; revised methodology targets removal of explicit documentation requirement from core preconditions.
Precondition 5 'Live quotes are available' should be dropped from core five per Precondition Reduction.
Precondition 4 is tester-capability phrasing ('tester can obtain') rather than pure system state.
[Valid:] partitions present on Actions 1–7 and align with buy-side intent.
Peculiarities define Q, LS, M, A, N_ask, C; B and N_bid appear in Actions but B/N_bid are contextual for counterfactual — acceptable if counterfactual moves to Peculiarities only.
Precondition 6 still requires Current Ask and Current Bid to differ (same as Ask ≠ Bid blocker in effect) — must not block execution.
Actions 8–11: Compute (8–9) and Compare (10–11) in Actions — CRITICAL violation of Action/Result separation.
Results assert both primary equality and counterfactual inequality; second bullet must move to Peculiarities optional note.
No conditional clauses in Results — PASS.
Actions 1–7 use Record/Select/Confirm/Input — no Compare/Compute in 1–7; steps 8–11 fail cognitive-free Actions rule.
Traceability: Test List shows `[AC-23]` only; AC-22 includes file path and fuller requirement anchor — align structure.
Summary length and Test Items detail differ from AC-22.
Side/field formatting: AC-23 uses backticks for Sell/Market in some actions; AC-22 uses bold for Buy/Market — minor presentation asymmetry.
Precondition and action step counts match AC-22 (7 preconditions, 11 actions) — partial structural parity.
Precondition 1: explicit confirm/document base currency — reduce per methodology.
Precondition 5: Live quotes — remove from core five.
Precondition 4: 'The tester can read' — not system-state-only phrasing.
[Valid:] partitions present on Actions 1–3, 5; step 4 omits explicit [Valid:] tag present in AC-22 step 4 pattern — slight inconsistency.
Peculiarities define Q, LS, M, B, N_bid, C; N_ask used in Action 9 but N_ask is not given the same definitional prominence as N_bid (methodology asks N_ask, N_bid defined — partial gap).

divergences: Traceability block layout and path detail differ (AC-22 file path + DX-WEB-097 section anchor vs AC-23 shortened lines).
Test Items: AC-22 lists read-only Order Value and Current Ask/Bid; AC-23 shorter.
Precondition 2: 'Order Entry is open' vs 'Order Entry popup is open'.
Markdown convention: **bold** vs `backticks` for side and order type in Actions.
Options section: different linked AC lists and ordering (acceptable content difference but not mirror-identical structure).
Peculiarities: AC-22 includes edge notes on Ask≠Bid counterfactual and AC-25 deferral; AC-23 different depth and missing symmetric edge notes.

immediate_fixes: Remove Ask≠Bid (or equivalent 'differ so that notionals differ') from blocking Preconditions; if useful, add optional non-blocking note under Peculiarities.

Collapse core Preconditions to five: drop explicit documentation requirement phrasing from prec 1, remove 'Live quotes available' as a separate precondition, remove Ask≠Bid blocker; keep login/base currency, OE new order, instrument family, access to Q/LS/M sources, Market + side.

Rewrite Actions to end at step 7 with only UI interaction and simultaneous UI reads (record) if still allowed as non-cognitive; move all compute, conversion, formatting, and comparison into Results as expected outcomes.
Results: single primary assertion (Order Value matches ask-based expected for AC-22 / bid-based for AC-23); relocate counterfactual inequality to Peculiarities as optional explanation only.
Normalize AC-23 Traceability to match AC-22 (paths, requirement line, Market Sell section anchor).
Align Test Items, precondition wording (obtain vs read, Order Entry phrasing), and markdown styling (**Order Value**, side labels) across AC-22 and AC-23.
In AC-23 Peculiarities, add explicit N_ask definition/formula mirroring how AC-22 presents N_ask for symmetry.
deferred_items: Decide whether 'record at same instant' steps 4–7 remain under Actions or move to a dedicated 'Observations' subsection if methodology tightens 'UI only' further.
Harmonize Options bullets to same ordering pattern where possible without losing case-specific references.
Optional: single template block for conversion C and tolerance language reused in both files.
